EventBus autoload as pub-sub hub consumed by HUD and WaveManager (entities emit, UI/systems listen)

nodes.label: Locked Decisions Table
nodes.rationale: Decisions frozen before coding/art because changing them later causes cascading rework across the whole project.

nodes.label: Base Resolution 640x360
nodes.rationale: Exact integer scaling to 720p (x2), 1080p (x3), 1440p (x4); changing later breaks all UI and framing.

nodes.label: Tile Size 16x16 px
nodes.rationale: Classic 16-bit style; all scenery art derives from this size, changing it means redoing all art.

nodes.label: Sprite Pivot: Bottom-Center
nodes.rationale: Required for Y-sort to work correctly (what is 'behind' renders first).

nodes.label: Texture Filter: Nearest
nodes.rationale: Nearest filter keeps pixel art crisp instead of blurred.

nodes.label: Movement Only in _physics_process
nodes.rationale: Mixing movement code with _process causes jitter.

nodes.label: Collision Layers (world, player, enemies, hurtboxes, pickups)
nodes.rationale: Physical body (blocking) and hurtbox (receiving damage) must always be separate areas; never reuse the body's CollisionShape for damage.
nodes.norm_label: collision layers (world, player, enemies, hurtboxes, pickups)

nodes.label: Input Actions Convention (move_click, skill_1..4, attack)
nodes.rationale: Code never references a physical key directly, only Input.is_action_pressed(name), so changing control scheme or adding gamepad support touches no scripts.

nodes.label: EventBus Autoload (global signals)
nodes.rationale: UI and systems listen to EventBus; entities emit. HUD must never hold a direct reference to the player node.

nodes.label: Stats Pipeline Formula ((base+flats)x(1+percents))
nodes.rationale: Defining the flat-vs-percent stacking formula upfront avoids the classic bug where a +10% item does not interact correctly with a +5 flat item.

nodes.label: Data as Resources (.tres, never hardcoded)
nodes.rationale: New content = new .tres file with no code changes; balancing becomes editing data files instead of hunting numbers in scripts.

nodes.label: Code Conventions (naming, unique names, one component per file)
nodes.norm_label: code conventions (naming, unique names, one component per file)

nodes.label: Save System Plan (JSON at user://save.json)
nodes.rationale: Design stats/inventory/progress as serializable pure data from the start rather than scattered across nodes.

nodes.label: Pause System Plan (get_tree().paused + process_mode)
nodes.rationale: Set up correct process_mode on UI nodes from the first scene, otherwise it becomes a bug hunt later.

nodes.label: Single Color Palette Decision
nodes.rationale: Mixing palettes is what makes an indie game look amateur; choose one palette (e.g. DB32/Resurrect) before the first sprite.

nodes.label: Missing 'id' in ext_resource tag (root cause)
nodes.rationale: Godot 4's text scene format requires every [ext_resource] tag to have an id, referenced in-body via ExtResource(id). This happens when .tscn files are hand-written or generated externally instead of saved by the Godot editor, which always writes id/load_steps automatically. It is the root cause of most of the reported error cascade.

nodes.label: Scenes Affected by Missing-ID Bug (8 .tscn files)
nodes.norm_label: scenes affected by missing-id bug (8 .tscn files)

nodes.label: Cascading Load Failures
nodes.rationale: Most of the ~30 reported errors were domino effects of the 8 malformed ext_resource tags, not independent problems.

nodes.label: const X = preload(scene) Pattern Breaking on Load Failure
nodes.norm_label: const x = preload(scene) pattern breaking on load failure

nodes.label: main.tscn Marked Corrupt via ArmorMenu Dependency
nodes.norm_label: main.tscn marked corrupt via armormenu dependency

nodes.label: Recommended Fix: Input Map Actions Instead of Raw Keys
nodes.rationale: Not auto-fixed because it requires a design decision: create actions in Project Settings > Input Map and use Input.is_action_just_pressed, or handle raw keys inside _input(event).
nodes.norm_label: recommended fix: input map actions instead of raw keys

nodes.label: DROP_MODE_ON_ITEM not found in base Control
nodes.norm_label: drop_mode_on_item not found in base control

nodes.label: get_global_mouse_position() not found in base self
nodes.norm_label: get_global_mouse_position() not found in base self

nodes.label: Design Pillars (Combat, Loot, Hordes, Dark Fantasy)
nodes.norm_label: design pillars (combat, loot, hordes, dark fantasy)

nodes.label: Composition via Reusable Components
nodes.rationale: Reusable components (HealthComponent, Hitbox/HurtboxComponent, StatsComponent, StateMachine) as child nodes instead of heavy inheritance, chosen to keep the codebase scalable.

nodes.label: SkillData Resource Class
nodes.rationale: Lets the team create 50 skills/items without writing 50 scripts.

nodes.label: Click-to-Move Decision (right mouse button)
nodes.rationale: Decided for Diablo/LoL feel, freeing keys for skills (QWER + 1-4). Obstacle avoidance via NavigationAgent2D deferred to M1 alongside enemy pathfinding.

nodes.label: Glow Visual Technique (HDR+Glow, PointLight2D, particles/shaders)
nodes.rationale: Golden rule of 'game juice': every hit needs flash + particle + sound + shake + knockback to look AAA even in pixel art.

nodes.label: Assets and Tools (Aseprite, Kenney, freesound, Tiled)
nodes.norm_label: assets and tools (aseprite, kenney, freesound, tiled)

nodes.label: Continuous Large Exploration World Decision
nodes.rationale: World is one large continuous map with multiple zones (crypt, dark forest, ruins) rather than separate levels; camera limits and spawners must come from the map, not be hardcoded to the player.

nodes.label: Open Risks: Netcode, Scope, Vertical Slice
nodes.rationale: Singleplayer first, multiplayer deferred but decoupled via EventBus since it is ~10x the work. Realistic first-milestone scope fixed at one map, one player, 2 enemies, 3 skills, basic loot.
nodes.norm_label: open risks: netcode, scope, vertical slice

nodes.label: Godot 4.x Standard (Engine)
nodes.rationale: Start with the Standard (GDScript) build; only switch to the .NET build if C# is later needed for performance.

nodes.label: Minimal Setup to Start Today (M0)
nodes.rationale: Only Godot Engine + GitHub CLI are required to begin the first M0 prototype; everything else can wait.

nodes.label: Save System Left for the End
nodes.norm_label: save system left for the end

nodes.label: Pathfinding Every Frame for All Enemies
nodes.norm_label: pathfinding every frame for all enemies

nodes.label: Infinite Scope Creep
nodes.rationale: The #1 killer of indie projects; nothing new enters scope until the vertical slice defined in GAME_DESIGN.md runs.

nodes.label: Multiplayer 'We'll Add It Later'
nodes.rationale: Retrofitting netcode onto a singleplayer game means rewriting it; the project is consciously singleplayer per GAME_DESIGN.md.

nodes.label: LOD Project Overview (ARPG, Diablo x LoL)
nodes.norm_label: lod project overview (arpg, diablo x lol)

nodes.label: Teal Spectral Energy / Glowing Cracks Motif
nodes.norm_label: teal spectral energy / glowing cracks motif

nodes.label: Four Elements Iconography (Fire, Water, Air, Earth)
nodes.norm_label: four elements iconography (fire, water, air, earth)

nodes.label: Ornate Dark-Stone Skill Icon Frame Style
nodes.norm_label: ornate dark-stone skill icon frame style

nodes.label: Meteor Storm / Rain of Fire motif
nodes.norm_label: meteor storm / rain of fire motif
